## [00:00 - 01:32]

This is the Pubcast Shot. I'm John Loomer. Today we're going to ask how are these changes to AEM possible? Let's go. Aggregated event measurement is changing and I have questions. The big news is that meta advertisers will no longer need to configure and rank their 8 web events or verify their domains going forward. While this is good news generally, AEM isn't going away. AEM is Meta's protocol for handling iOS opt-outs. Previously, meta needed advertisers to configure and rank their 8 events to stay within Apple's rules. So, how is this even possible? I assume that meta has enough data now because of machine learning and can sort it all out itself? I don't know. And maybe most importantly, how will it impact results? I just can't imagine meta making this huge structural change if it would lead to bad results. Knowingly, at least. Unlike the original changes related to iOS 14, meta wasn't forced into this change. So will attribution be affected? Will it get better? It'd be really nice to have an official statement from meta. So this shot was taken directly from a video I did on TikTok. If you're not already following me on TikTok, do so at John Loomer. You can also keep following me here. Don't forget to subscribe, rate and review. Until next time, do awesome things. I'm out.
